docs.title: Slime Dungeon
docs.text:
A fast-paced dungeon crawler where you battle hundreds of slimy enemies, collect cursed gear, and try not to die on floor 2.

Get Started View Characters

docs.title: What is Slime Dungeon?
docs.text:
Slime Dungeon is a roguelike dungeon crawler with procedurally generated levels, a cast of four very different characters, and an overwhelming slime problem. Each run is different. Most runs end badly. That's fine.

  • 4 playable characters with unique abilities
  • 10 floors of increasingly angry slimes
  • Local co-op for up to 4 players
  • Full permadeath (no saves, no mercy)
  • Unlockable curses that make the game harder for fun

docs.title: At a glance
docs.text:
Detail Info Genre Roguelike dungeon crawler Players 1–4 local co-op Platforms PC, Mac, Linux Difficulty Medium → Brutal Slime count Way too many

First time?

Start with the Knight. Slow, tanky, forgiving. Save the Rogue for when you know what you're doing.

docs.title: About
docs.text: Slime Dungeon was developed by a two-person team over the course of one very long weekend. It started as a game jam entry, somehow won third place, and then spiralled into a real project.

docs.title: The team
docs.text: Name Role Mathias Programming, level design Teodor Pixel art, sound design, vibes

docs.title: Frequently asked questions
docs.text:
Why is there no save system? Permadeath is a core design choice. Saves would undermine the tension that makes each run feel meaningful. Also we didn't have time to implement it during the jam.

Will you add more characters? The Necromancer and Berserker are already in. Possibly a Paladin at some point. No promises.

The Bard feels weak. The Bard is weak in solo. That's intentional. Play co-op or accept the challenge.

I found a bug. Open an issue on GitHub. Include your floor number, character, and what passive items you had.

docs.title: Built with
docs.text:
• Godot Engine 4 — game engine
  • Aseprite — pixel art
  • MkDocs Material — this docs site
  • mkdocs-callouts — Obsidian-style callout syntax

Thanks for playing

If Slime Dungeon ate three hours of your evening, mission accomplished.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Download the zip from the releases page, extract it, and run the executable. No installer needed.

# Linux / Mac — run from terminal
chmod +x slimedungeon
./slimedungeon

# Or just double-click the .exe on Windows like a normal person

System requirements

Basically any machine from the last 10 years will run this. It's pixel art.

docs.title: Controls
docs.text:
Action Keyboard Controller Move WASD / Arrows Left stick Attack Space A Dodge roll Shift B Use item E Y Pause / menu Esc Start

Controller support

Xbox and PlayStation controllers work out of the box. Generic USB controllers may not be detected — use keyboard if yours isn't recognised.

docs.title: Your first run
docs.text:
1. Pick a character — see the Characters page for a full breakdown.
  2. Floor 1 is always the same — learn the layout before experimenting with builds.
  3. Pick up everything — even bad items can be sold to the shop on floor 3.
  4. Don't stand in slime puddles. They deal damage every tick. Yes, even the small ones.

Permadeath is real

There are no saves and no checkpoints. When you die, the run ends. Your gold, gear, and progress are gone. This is intentional. Embrace it.

docs.title: The shop
docs.text:
The shop appears at the end of every third floor. You can:

  • Buy health potions (40g each, carry up to 3)
  • Reroll your passive item once per visit (25g)
  • Unlock a curse for bonus gold drops (not recommended on your first run)

Saving gold

Don't blow all your gold on potions. A strong passive item from the shop often wins more runs than full HP.

docs.title: Enemy types
docs.text:
Every floor introduces new enemy variants. Learning their movement patterns is the difference between a smooth run and an embarrassing death on floor 2.

Enemy Floor HP Behaviour Green Slime 1+ 20 Moves slowly toward the player Blue Slime 2+ 35 Splits into two small slimes on death Spike Slime 3+ 45 Shoots a spike projectile every 2 seconds King Slime 5+ 80 Charges at full speed when HP drops below 50% Void Slime 8+ 120 Teleports randomly; ignores walls

Blue Slimes

Always kill Blue Slimes last. The two small slimes they split into can chain into more splits if you have bad AoE timing.

docs.title: Boss fights
docs.text: A boss spawns at the end of floors 5 and 10.

docs.title: Floor 5 — The Slime King
docs.text:
The Slime King has three phases:

  1. Phase 1 (100–60% HP): Slow charge attacks. Dodge sideways.
  2. Phase 2 (60–30% HP): Spawns 4 Green Slimes every 10 seconds.
  3. Phase 3 (30–0% HP): Enrages — moves at 2× speed, spawns continuously.
Phase 3 damage formula:
  base_damage × 2 + (active_slimes × 0.5)

Don't let slimes pile up

In phase 3, clear the spawned slimes first or their combined contact damage will shred even the Knight.

docs.title: Floor 10 — The Void Amalgamation
docs.text:
The final boss. It combines every slime type into one chaotic encounter.

No spoilers

Figuring out the Void Amalgamation's pattern yourself is part of the experience. There's a reason the achievement is called "Did you even read the docs?"

docs.title: Tips for deep runs
docs.text:
• Dodge roll has i-frames — you are invincible during the animation. Use it aggressively.
  • The shop on floor 6 has a secret item if you have exactly 0 gold when you enter.
  • Passive items stack — two Iron Skins gives double the armour bonus.
  • Standing still for 3 seconds regenerates 5 HP. This is often more useful than potions on low-HP characters.

The 0-gold trick

Spend every coin before floor 6 and the shopkeeper will respect your poverty with a Cursed Rabbit's Foot — arguably the best item in the game.

docs.title: Characters
docs.text: Slime Dungeon has four playable characters. Each has different stats, a unique special ability, and a completely different feel. Pick whoever matches your playstyle — or whoever you suffer least with.

docs.title: Stats overview
docs.text: Character  HP  Speed  Attack Special Knight ●●●●●●●● ●●●●○○○○ ●●●●●●○○ Shield Bash Rogue ●●●●●○○○ ●●●●●●●● ●●●●○○○○ Backstab Wizard ●●●●○○○○ ●●●●●●○○ ●●●●●●●● Fireball Bard ●●●●●●○○ ●●●●●●○○ ●●●○○○○○ Rally Cry

docs.title: Character details
docs.text:
KnightRogueWizardBard

Role: Tank / Front-line brawler

The Knight is slow, sturdy, and satisfying to play. Shield Bash stuns nearby enemies for 1.5 seconds — great for interrupting boss attacks and getting out of bad situations.

Best passive items: Iron Skin, Thorns Amulet, Bloodstone

Beginner pick

The Knight is the most forgiving character in the game. If you're new, start here.

Role: Burst damage / Glass cannon

The Rogue is blindingly fast and deals massive damage when attacking from behind with Backstab (2× multiplier). Dies in three hits.

Best passive items: Shadow Cloak, Lucky Coin, Poison Blade

Don't get surrounded

If three or more slimes pin you against a wall, you're dead before you can dodge. Always keep an escape route open.

Role: Area damage / Crowd control

Fireball hits everything on screen. Devastating against groups, overkill against single targets. Limited mobility means positioning is everything.

Best passive items: Mana Crystal, Spell Echo, Glass Orb

Glass cannon

The Wizard has the lowest HP in the game. One bad floor can end a great run instantly.

Role: Support / Buffer

Rally Cry boosts all nearby allies' speed and attack damage by 30% for 5 seconds. Incredible in co-op. Mediocre alone. Unlocks the "Emotional Support" achievement if you clear floor 10 solo.

Bard solo runs

Bard solo is a self-imposed challenge. There are achievements for it. You will suffer, but you will also feel very cool.

docs.title: Unlockable characters
docs.text:
Two additional characters are unlocked by completing specific achievements:

Character Unlock condition Necromancer Reach floor 8 with the Wizard Berserker Die 20 times total (any character)

Note

Unlockable characters are harder than the base four. Don't chase them on your first day.
